title: Add E2E training test for bilinear interpolate-based upsampler
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression test that wires Conv2d + bilinear interpolate (upsampling 2x) + MSE into an Adam training loop. Validates the recently fixed tensor_interpolate bilinear autograd path (frankentorch-3t1t) end-to-end through a learnable conv with the upsampling chain.

Setup:
- 1x1x2x2 learnable input
- conv2d (1->1, 3x3, padding 1) → still 1x1x2x2
- interpolate (bilinear, scale 2) → 1x1x4x4
- MSE against target 4x4 ones tensor
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: Add STFT and ISTFT for spectral/signal processing
close_reason: Completed

title: tensor_pdist severs autograd; composable through index_select for finite p > 0
description:
tensor_pdist at lib.rs:4349 computes pairwise L_p distances between rows of a 2-D input. For finite p > 0 the math is composable through autograd-aware primitives (index_select pairs of rows, abs, pow, sum_dim, pow). For p = inf it's argmax-routing; for p = 0 it's a non-differentiable counting metric.

Strategy: build row_i and row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 each, use tensor_index_select to gather row pairs, then compute:
   diff = left - right
   per_pair = (sum_k |diff[k]|^p)^(1/p)
All ops autograd-aware. Add fail-loud guards for p == 0 (counting metric, non-diff) and p == +inf (could be added with max_dim but defer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. For finite p > 0, composed tensor_pdist through index_select + sub + abs + pow + sum_dim + pow primitives. Pre-built row_i / row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 enumerate the upper-triangular pairs; index_select gathers the row-pair tensors so subsequent ops broadcast naturally. p == 0 (counting metric) and p == +inf (max-routing) now fail-loud on requires_grad=true rather than silently severing — autograd implementations for those cases are deferred. Added pdist_l2_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test. ft-api lib tests: 1169.

title: Harden runtime durability envelope metadata normalization
close_reason: Completed

title: Bound conformance parser diagnostics for fuzzed string inputs
description: testing-fuzzing audit found unbounded diagnostic echo in crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs parser helpers. parse_binary_op, parse_dtype, parse_device, and parse_keyset include the full rejected input in error strings. These functions consume values from JSON conformance fixtures and fuzzed/adversarial inputs; a single oversized token can produce multi-megabyte error/log output even though the harness already has bounded_diagnostic for legacy oracle errors. Fix by bounding rejected parser tokens through the existing bounded_diagnostic helper, add regression tests with oversized strings, and preserve existing valid parse behavior.
close_reason: Bounded conformance parser diagnostics for oversized fuzzed tokens

title: tensor_kthvalue severs autograd; index-routing backward needed
description:
tensor_kthvalue at lib.rs:12512 returns the k-th smallest element of a 1-D tensor. The value branch is just input[idx] where idx is the rank-k sorted index. PyTorch's torch.kthvalue IS differentiable: grad_input[idx] = grad_value, all other positions zero.

Wrap value branch in tensor_apply_function with this single-index routing backward. Index branch stays a non-grad leaf (integer).
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_kthvalue's value branch in tensor_apply_function with single-index routing backward: grad_input[idx] = grad_value where idx is the sort-rank-k index. The index branch stays a non-grad leaf. Added kthvalue_propagates_gradient_to_routed_index regression test (x=[3,1,4,1,5], k=3 → 3rd smallest is at idx 0 with value 3, grad_input = [1,0,0,0,0]). ft-api lib tests: 1163.

title: Add gradient clipping utilities: clip_grad_norm_, clip_grad_value_

title: Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops
description:
## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries

### Tests (11 core sparse + 177 optim + 299 core/kernel passing)
- 8 in ft-core: COO/CSR creation, to_dense roundtrip, error handling
- 3 in ft-kernel-cpu: matmul, coalesce, add
- All existing tests pass with new sparse gradient infrastructure

### Remaining for full sparse gradient flow
- Wire sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
- Test sparse gradient round-trip with Embedding + SparseAdam
close_reason: Wired sparse backward path in IndexSelect (sparse_dim=1, dim=0) — Embedding(sparse=true) now surfaces SparseCOOTensor on TensorBackwardReport.sparse_gradient(weight); SparseAdam updates only touched rows; full Embedding+SparseAdam roundtrip test added in ft-optim; 4 sparse-grad tests added across ft-nn/ft-optim, all passing.

title: porting: add tensor_view_as for torch.Tensor.view_as parity
description: torch.Tensor.view_as(other) reshapes input to the shape of other. Trivial wrapper around tensor_view (same as reshape). Useful for porting code that uses view_as for shape-matching.
close_reason: Added tensor_view_as in ft-api as a thin wrapper over tensor_view that takes a reference tensor instead of an explicit shape. Strict view (element counts must match), not a broadcast. Autograd-aware via the underlying view. 2 unit tests cover shape propagation and gradient routing. ft-api now at 1292 lib tests.

title: [profiling-software-performance] tensor_softshrink has three redundant full(shape, lambd) allocations
description:
Same compose-anti-pattern family as cc2o (heaviside/pow_tensor), d521 (sinc), 4w7g (atanh/focal_loss), and x8r1 (entr). tensor_softshrink at ft-api lib.rs:5375 allocates the same lambd-valued tensor THREE times within a single forward call:

  let lambd_t      = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_gt mask
  let lambd_const  = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_sub
  let lambd_const2 = full(shape, lambd)?;     // for tensor_add

All three are operands of read-only ops (tensor_gt / tensor_sub / tensor_add); one shared lambd tensor suffices.

Net per-call savings: -2 full() allocations + -2 autograd-tape nodes. tensor_softshrink is a torch activation function used in sparse-coding / wavelet thresholding heads, so the wasted work compounds in training.

Acceptance:
- tensor_softshrink reuses one shared lambd tensor across gt mask, sub, and add call sites
- existing softshrink tests still pass — no behavior change
close_reason: Shipped: 4c0eacf. tensor_softshrink shares one lambd_t across the gt mask, the sub-shift, and the add-shift. -2 full() + -2 tape nodes per call. 2 softshrink tests including gradient regression pass.

title: Align proposed architecture crate map with current workspace layers
description: Update PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md so its crate map and runtime flow mention the in-tree ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data layers instead of stopping at ft-api/ft-conformance.
notes: Updated the proposed architecture doc so the crate map includes ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and the runtime flow now acknowledges those higher-level layers before conformance/durability.

title: Metamorphic property test: det(AB) = det(A) * det(B)
description: Now that tensor_linalg_det is autograd-aware (frankentorch-pvfk), add a metamorphic property test that locks the multiplicative invariant det(A @ B) = det(A) * det(B) on small (2x2 / 3x3) random matrices. Exercises the new TensorNodeId-returning code path through composition with tensor_matmul and tensor_mul, surfacing any regressions in the kernel or the autograd-aware shim.
close_reason: Added fuzz_metamorphic_det_of_product_equals_product_of_dets — proptest with 2x2/3x3 random matrices (entries bounded by ±128/23 ≈ ±5.6). Asserts |det(AB) - det(A)*det(B)| within a Hadamard-style bound proportional to (n*|entry|max)^n * n^2 * EPSILON. Exercises tensor_linalg_det's autograd-aware code path (added under pvfk) through composition with tensor_matmul. ft-conformance now at 189 fuzz tests (up from 188).

title: Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild
description:
## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence
close_reason: Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption

title: porting: add tensor_matrix_transpose for torch.Tensor.mT parity
description: torch.Tensor.mT (and torch.transpose(x, -2, -1)) is the standard 'transpose last two dims' op, works on any rank ≥ 2. Common in batched matmul code where you can't use the 2-arg tensor_transpose easily without computing rank. Add it as a thin wrapper that resolves to tensor_transpose(input, ndim-2, ndim-1).
close_reason: Added tensor_matrix_transpose in ft-api: transpose of last two dims for any rank ≥ 2. Composes via tensor_transpose(input, ndim-2, ndim-1); rejects rank < 2 with a clear error. Equivalent to torch.Tensor.mT and torch.transpose(x, -2, -1). Common in batched matmul code where the rank may not be known at the call site. 5 unit tests cover 2-D row/col swap, batched 3-D forward correctness, double-apply identity, rank-below-2 rejection (1-D and 0-D), and gradient propagation. ft-api now at 1333 lib tests. Also re-closes frankentorch-pvfk and frankentorch-3v6e in JSONL — they keep getting reverted to open by other agents' br operations writing through the drifted DB.

title: Enforce index_add/index_copy shape + rank rules
description: Audit: tensor_index_add/index_copy accept mismatched src/index shapes and non-1D indices; they silently ignore extra indices. Fix by enforcing index rank=1 and src shape matches input except dim, with src_dim == index length. Add tests for mismatch errors and f32 parity.
close_reason: Completed

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] no torch oracle for tensor_diag (1-D embed and 2-D extract paths)
description:
tensor_diag (added in 5a11cb5) dispatches by rank — 1-D becomes a 2-D matrix with input on the requested diagonal, 2-D extracts the diagonal as a 1-D tensor. Six lib unit tests cover the dispatch logic. No subprocess oracle pins the actual upstream torch.diag values for non-zero offsets, where the indexing semantics are easy to get subtly wrong (off-by-one between super and sub diagonals, off-by-one between 1-D embed offset and the resulting matrix size).

Concrete repro: a regression to tensor_diag_embed offset arithmetic that flips super/sub direction would still pass the lib unit test (the direction is consistent within tensor_diag_embed) but diverge from torch. The oracle catches that.

Fix direction: add torch_diag_subprocess_conformance with a fixed grid of (input, diagonal) pairs that exercises:
  - 1-D embed at offset 0 (main), +1 (super), -1 (sub)
  - 2-D extract at offset 0 (main), +1 (super), -1 (sub)
  - rectangular 2-D extraction (m != n)
For each case, run torch.diag on the same input/offset and assert ft-api returns the same shape and bit-equal values.

Acceptance:
- new test torch_diag_subprocess_conformance using the existing torch_*_subprocess_conformance pattern
- 6+ cases covering both rank dispatch paths
- skips silently if torch unavailable
- ft-conformance lib tests +1
close_reason: Shipped: 7224820. torch_diag_subprocess_conformance pins both rank-dispatch paths (1-D embed and 2-D extract) across main/super/sub diagonals + rectangular 2-D case. 8 cases, bit-equal via to_bits().

title: porting: add tensor_take + tensor_swapaxes/swapdims for torch parity
description: torch.take(input, indices) returns input.flatten()[indices], reshaped like indices. Useful for advanced indexing (the same primitive numpy.take provides). torch.swapaxes / torch.swapdims are direct aliases for transpose (different name conventions for numpy compatibility). Add tensor_take by composing tensor_reshape (flatten) + tensor_index_select; add swapaxes/swapdims as one-line wrappers around tensor_transpose.
close_reason: Added tensor_take, tensor_swapaxes, and tensor_swapdims for torch parity. tensor_take(input, indices) = input.flatten()[indices].reshape(indices.shape) — composes through tensor_reshape (flatten input + flatten indices) + tensor_index_select + tensor_reshape (back to indices shape); autograd-aware via index_select backward including repeated-index gradient accumulation. swapaxes/swapdims are one-line aliases for tensor_transpose (numpy compat names). 4 unit tests cover forward correctness, gradient routing, repeated-index accumulation, and alias equivalence to transpose. ft-api now at 1245 lib tests.

title: Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes
description: Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.
close_reason: Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.

title: Fix ft-nn MultiMarginLoss autograd via one_hot + relu + pow [child of cq0b]
description:
ft-nn MultiMarginLoss::forward at lib.rs:11852 has the same per-sample severance pattern as the session multi_margin_loss method (already fixed under frankentorch-x9cr).

Compose through session.one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div for per-sample loss, then apply the Reduction enum's mean/sum/none.
notes: Fixed via composition through one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div primitives — same recipe as session multi_margin_loss (frankentorch-x9cr) but with the Reduction enum dispatch at the end. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp
description: These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.
notes: All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.

title: Add E2E training test for GroupNorm + Conv2d block
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression that wires a Conv2d → GroupNorm block into a small image-regression task. Validates the GroupNorm composition (reshape + mean_dim + sub + mul + sqrt + div + affine) end-to-end through Adam.

Setup:
- 1x4x4x4 input (batch=1, channels=4, H=4, W=4)
- Conv2d(4, 4, 3x3, padding 1)
- GroupNorm(num_groups=2, num_channels=4)
- MSE against target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.5 (relaxed because GroupNorm normalizes)

title: [parity-gap] ConvTranspose1d module output_padding parity
close_reason: Implemented ConvTranspose1d output_padding parity

title: porting: add tensor_mv (matrix-vector multiply) for torch.mv parity
description: torch.mv(M, v) returns M @ v for a 2-D matrix M (shape [m, n]) and 1-D vector v (shape [n]); output is 1-D of shape [m]. PyTorch has it as a dedicated op even though matmul covers the same shape; user code calls it directly. Compose through autograd-aware primitives: reshape v to [n, 1] → tensor_matmul(M, v) → reshape to [m]. Add a few correctness and gradient regression tests.
close_reason: Added pub fn tensor_mv(M, v) at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs — matrix-vector product M @ v matching torch.mv. Composes through tensor_reshape + tensor_matmul + tensor_reshape; autograd-aware on both inputs. Added 3 unit tests: mv_known_values (forward correctness), mv_propagates_gradient_to_matrix_and_vector (analytic gradient regression for sum(M @ v)), mv_rejects_wrong_ranks (fail-loud for 1-D M, 2-D v, mismatched n). ft-api now at 1211 lib tests (up from 1208).

title: Numerical gradient check: tensor_linalg_det backward via finite differences
description: Lock the new autograd-aware tensor_linalg_det backward (added under frankentorch-pvfk) against numerical Jacobian computed by central finite differences. For each entry A[i,j], perturb ±eps, recompute det, and confirm (det(A+) - det(A-)) / (2*eps) matches the analytic backward to within finite-difference truncation tolerance. Catches any sign flip, transpose error, or scaling bug in the Jacobi-formula backward.
close_reason: Added linalg_det_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — exercises the new tensor_linalg_det backward (pvfk) on 4 well-conditioned 2x2/3x3 matrices (diag, general, rotation, perturbed-identity). Compares analytic ∂det/∂A from FrankenTorch's autograd against central FD with eps=1e-6 and abs_tol=5e-5. Locks the Jacobi-formula backward against any sign/transpose/scaling regression. ft-conformance now at 148 base lib tests, 194 with --features fuzz.

title: Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)
description:
Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward
close_reason: Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.

title: porting: add causal_mask helper for masked attention
description: Mirror torch.nn.Transformer.generate_square_subsequent_mask. Returns a [seq_len, seq_len] tensor with 0.0 on and below the main diagonal and -inf above. Used to mask future tokens in transformer decoder self-attention. Currently every model that needs this builds the mask manually in its own forward; a shared helper makes for cleaner ports of HuggingFace / fairseq style code.
close_reason: Added FrankenTorchSession::causal_mask(size) helper mirroring torch.nn.Transformer.generate_square_subsequent_mask. Returns [size, size] tensor with -inf above diagonal and 0.0 on/below. Designed for use as additive mask in masked attention: softmax(scores + causal_mask). 3 unit tests cover the diagonal layout, zero-size edge case (returns [0, 0]), and the canonical softmax-zeros-future-positions usage pattern. ft-api now at 1260 lib tests.

title: Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll
description:
Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.
notes: All children closed: poisson_nll (zl3n, 8d33f51), gaussian_nll (vjow, this commit), focal_loss (m6uk, this commit).

title: Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm
description: Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.
close_reason: slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.

title: Wire f32 tensor comparisons and float classify to dispatch
close_reason: Completed

title: porting: add tensor_asinh / tensor_acosh / tensor_atanh for torch parity
description:
torch.asinh / acosh / atanh — inverse hyperbolic functions. Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
  asinh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x^2 + 1))
  acosh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x^2 - 1))   (x >= 1)
  atanh(x) = 0.5 * log((1 + x) / (1 - x))   (|x| < 1)
Gradient-correct since all sub-ops are on the autograd tape. Useful for normalizing-flow models, conformal maps, and porting numpy code.
close_reason: Added tensor_asinh / acosh / atanh in ft-api. Closed-form compositions: asinh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x² + 1)); acosh(x) = log(x + sqrt(x² - 1)) (defined for x >= 1); atanh(x) = 0.5 * log((1+x)/(1-x)) (defined for |x| < 1). All autograd-aware via existing primitives. 5 unit tests cover known values (asinh(sinh(t)) = t round-trip etc.) and analytic gradient regression for asinh and atanh (d/dx asinh = 1/sqrt(1+x²), d/dx atanh = 1/(1-x²)). ft-api now at 1250 lib tests.

title: Activation functions lack subprocess conformance: sigmoid, tanh, silu, mish
description:
Existing subprocess conformance suite covers gelu, softplus, exp/log/expm1/log1p/erf/erfc/lgamma/trig/hyperbolic, but the workhorse activations sigmoid / tanh / silu / mish have no scipy or libm reference comparison. PyTorch parity for these matters because:
- sigmoid (= 1/(1+exp(-x)) = expit) is the building block for binary classification, gating, attention
- tanh is the workhorse for RNN/LSTM
- silu/swish (= x*sigmoid(x)) is used in transformer FFN
- mish (= x*tanh(softplus(x))) is used in detection / vision

Reference: scipy.special.expit (sigmoid), numpy.tanh, numpy compositions for silu and mish. ULP-tolerant comparison; FrankenTorch composes these through libm exp/tanh which should match within ~1 ULP on smooth interior plus edge cases for ±inf / NaN / large-magnitude saturation.

Add torch_activations_libm_subprocess_conformance covering all four with ~30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / large / Inf / NaN. Filed during testing-conformance pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Added torch_activations_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering sigmoid (vs scipy.special.expit), tanh (vs numpy.tanh), silu = x*sigmoid(x), and mish = x*tanh(softplus(x)) across 30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / saturation (±50, ±100, ±700) / Inf / NaN. ULP-tolerant comparator: 4 ULP for sigmoid/tanh, 16 ULP for the composed silu/mish. Subprocess conformance count now 33 (was 32).

title: Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems
description:
FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.
close_reason: Already completed by another agent: Parity Scope Declaration section added, Full PyTorch drop-in changed to parity_gap, Deferred Subsystems section with 9 entries and tracking beads, Gap Policy updated. Verified all referenced beads exist and tensor_apply_function claim is accurate.

title: Capture committed perf-SLO baseline artifact for G5
description:
Follow-up to frankentorch-vws.

The perf-SLO runner/gate now exists in ft-conformance, strict cargo gates pass, and we captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement in artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_report_full_once.json plus PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md. The current result shows all five direct spec budgets failing and the three regression budgets remaining baseline_required without a committed baseline.

Remaining work:
1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with the intended statistical envelope (default 100 iterations, warmups as configured) on a suitable worker/host.
2. Commit a stable baseline artifact at the canonical perf_slo baseline path for regression comparisons.
3. Decide whether the single-sample full_once artifact should stay as an exploratory snapshot or be superseded by the committed baseline bundle.
4. Hand the baseline path/contract to frankentorch-ioo so G5 can call run_perf_slos + check_perf_slos deterministically in CI.

Notes:
- rch exec validates runtime execution but does not automatically persist generated artifacts back into the local worktree across separate invocations, so baseline capture should use a workflow that intentionally preserves the produced artifact bundle.
- Direct spec-budget failures from the single-sample full profile are already documented in artifacts/phase2c/performance/PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md.
close_reason: Normalized the canonical perf-SLO measurement/baseline paths, committed perf_slo_baseline_v1.json plus PERF_SLO_BASELINE_V1.md, and aligned runner/checker defaults so G5 has a stable baseline contract. Higher-iteration replacement baseline tracked separately.

title: Add E2E training test for vanilla RNN final-hidden-state regression
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for vanilla RNN. Mirrors the LSTM (eets) and GRU (c6a4) E2E tests but exercises the RNNCell forward + tanh activation path.

Setup:
- RNN(input_size=4, hidden_size=4, num_layers=1, bidirectional=false, dropout=0.0, batch_first=false)
- 3x1x4 input
- MSE against zeros target [1, 4]
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: tensor_interpolate linear/bilinear/trilinear autograd via index_select + weighted sum
description:
After frankentorch-ov0v added a fail-loud guard, tensor_interpolate with mode='linear'/'bilinear'/'trilinear' is unusable in autograd flows. Each output position is a weighted sum of 2 (linear), 4 (bilinear), or 8 (trilinear) input pixels — compose via tensor_reshape + tensor_index_select (per corner) + tensor_mul (with non-grad per-position weight tensor) + tensor_add.

Bicubic uses 16 corners with cubic Hermite spline weights — defer.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_interpolate (line 14674) — interpolate_linear_1d, interpolate_bilinear, interpolate_trilinear paths.

title: Bilinear bias broadcast uses tensor_expand on wrong rank
description:
Bilinear::forward (line 1047) calls tensor_expand on bias (shape [out_features]) targeting [*, out_features]. tensor_expand requires the input rank to match the target — same bug pattern as ConvTranspose1d (frankentorch-ru7u). Use_bias=true would panic with ShapeMismatch.

Fix: tensor_reshape bias from [out_features] to a shape with the same rank as out_shape (1s in all leading dims, out_features in the last) before expanding.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs Bilinear::forward (line 1046+).

title: Audit dispatch/kernel CPU edge cases
description: Deep audit pass for ft-dispatch/ft-kernel-cpu execution flow; fix bugs/edge cases and add tests as needed.
close_reason: Fixed missing f32 test import; validated checks/tests.

title: Remaining ft-api ops with fail-loud autograd guards: stft, istft, affine_grid, grid_sample, complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex
description:
After closing frankentorch-1tax (statistical/special ops), 7 ops in ft-api still reject requires_grad=true inputs with hard errors instead of carrying gradients:

- stft (line 1248) — short-time Fourier transform; PyTorch's torch.stft supports backward via inverse FFT.
- istft (line 1411) — inverse short-time Fourier transform; symmetric to stft.
- stft window tensor (line 6733) — separate guard rejecting tracked window inputs.
- affine_grid (line 7774) — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.
- grid_sample (line 7905) — torch.nn.functional.grid_sample supports backward.
- complex (line 994/1001) — torch.complex(re, im) supports backward through real and imaginary inputs (Wirtinger derivatives).
- view_as_real / view_as_complex (lines 1101, 1145) — complex view ops also support gradients in PyTorch.

These are not 'severed-autograd' — they're explicit guards that fail-loud, so users can't accidentally lose gradients silently. But they DO block training paths that would work in PyTorch (e.g., audio models using STFT, vision models using affine_grid + grid_sample for spatial transformer networks).

Each child needs its own fix bead. Children grouped by domain:
  Audio:   stft + istft + stft-window
  Vision:  affine_grid + grid_sample
  Complex: complex + view_as_real + view_as_complex (deferred — Wirtinger calculus is non-trivial; PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize)

Priority for audio/vision (P2): real downstream training paths blocked. Priority for complex (P3): Wirtinger calculus is genuinely tricky and may need its own design doc first.
notes:
affine_grid slice closed in 669df96 (frankentorch-bfj5). Remaining children that still need work:
  - stft / istft / stft window — Audio. Backward via inverse FFT or windowed-overlap-add gradient. Non-trivial.
  - grid_sample (bilinear mode) — Vision. Backward via bilinear interpolation derivatives in both input and grid; nearest mode is non-differentiable in grid.
  - complex / view_as_real / view_as_complex — Wirtinger calculus. PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize; needs a design doc first.

Each remaining child should get its own bead when picked up. Priorities:
  - stft + istft + stft-window: P2 (audio training paths blocked)
  - grid_sample: P2 (vision training paths blocked, e.g. spatial transformer networks)
  - complex/Wirtinger: P3 (defer until design doc)
close_reason: All parking-lot children resolved in source: affine_grid (bfj5), grid_sample bilinear (0473840), stft/istft/tensor_complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex fail-loud guards locked by m6s7 regression test. Re-closing in raw JSONL — DO NOT run br operations on this file before commit (the dbp DB corruption reverts the state).

title: porting: add tensor_glu functional API for torch.nn.functional.glu
description: GLU (Gated Linear Unit) splits input in half along a dim and computes a * sigmoid(b). Already implemented as nn::Module GLU but missing as a top-level functional API. Compose via tensor_narrow + tensor_sigmoid + tensor_mul; autograd-aware. Common in transformer-style FFN blocks.
close_reason: Added tensor_glu functional API in ft-api. Composes via tensor_narrow + tensor_sigmoid + tensor_mul; autograd-aware. Signature: tensor_glu(input, dim) → out where out shape is half of input along dim. Validates dim in range and dim_size even. 5 unit tests cover 1-D forward, 2-D forward along dim=1, odd-size rejection, out-of-range dim rejection, and analytic gradient regression on both halves (∂/∂a = sigmoid(b), ∂/∂b = a * sigmoid(b) * (1 - sigmoid(b))). ft-api now at 1283 lib tests.

title: perfect-e2e: TransformerEncoder (2-layer) trains end-to-end with Adam
description: Add E2E perfect-e2e for TransformerEncoder — multi-layer stack of TransformerEncoderLayer. Currently the perfect-e2e battery covers a single TransformerEncoderLayer (dv5a) but not the full multi-layer wrapper. Validates layer-stacking gradient flow.

Locks the layer-stacking gradient flow that the single-layer dv5a test does not exercise. ft-conformance now at 153 base lib tests, 201 with --features fuzz.

title: Add basic quantization support (torch.quantization equivalent)
description:
PyTorch's quantization allows running models with INT8 weights for faster inference and smaller model size. This is increasingly important for edge deployment.

Implementation plan (CPU quantization only):
1. Add quantized dtypes to ft-core: QInt8, QUInt8 (with scale and zero_point metadata)
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta extending TensorMeta with quantization parameters
3. Add quantize/dequantize functions:
   - session.quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, dtype) -> quantized tensor
   - session.dequantize(quantized) -> float tensor
4. Add quantized linear layer (QuantizedLinear) in ft-nn:
   - Stores INT8 weights + F32 bias
   - Forward: dequantize weights -> matmul -> add bias (or fused quantized matmul if we add a kernel)
5. Add basic observer (MinMaxObserver) for calibration:
   - Tracks running min/max of tensor values
   - Computes optimal scale/zero_point
6. Add post-training quantization workflow:
   - Calibrate observers on representative data
   - Convert float model to quantized model

Tests:
- Unit test: quantize then dequantize recovers original values (within quantization error)
- Unit test: quantized linear forward matches dequantize-then-matmul
- Unit test: MinMaxObserver correctly tracks min/max
- Unit test: scale/zero_point calculation is correct
- E2E test: train a float model, quantize it, verify inference output is close to float model
- Edge case: quantize with scale=0 should error, not divide by zero
close_reason: Implemented MVP fake-quantization slice: tensor_quantize_per_tensor / tensor_dequantize_per_tensor / tensor_fake_quantize_per_tensor on FrankenTorchSession (composes round + clamp + scalar shift; STE backward via tensor_round); MinMaxObserver and QParams with qint8/quint8 helpers in ft-nn; calibrated observer -> qparams -> fake_quantize E2E test. Deferred scope (QInt8/QUInt8 storage dtypes, QuantizedLinear, per-channel quantization) tracked in follow-up bead.

title: Implement tensor_histc operation
description: Add histc (histogram computation) across the stack. Computes histogram of a tensor. PyTorch signature: torch.histc(input, bins=100, min=0, max=0). Used for data analysis and feature statistics.
close_reason: Implemented tensor_histc with auto-range, clamping, edge cases. 4 tests passing.

title: Add tests for ft-data sampler builder methods and edge cases

title: multilabel_soft_margin_loss severs autograd; delegate to bce_with_logits_loss
description:
multilabel_soft_margin_loss at lib.rs:10507 implements the per-element BCE-with-logits formula manually (max(x,0) - x*t + log1p(exp(-|x|))), accumulates in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed across the recent ft-api cleanup.

For a 2-D input [batch, classes] the multilabel_soft_margin_loss formula reduces to mean over all (i, j) of BCE_with_logits(x[i,j], t[i,j]), which is exactly what bce_with_logits_loss computes (the latter is autograd-aware via composition through tensor_relu + tensor_abs + tensor_log1p + tensor_mean).

Fix: delegate to self.bce_with_logits_loss(input, target) with the existing 2-D shape validation. Same arithmetic, same numerical stability via log1p(exp(-|x|)), but with the autograd tape preserved.

Sister of frankentorch-ilwr (cross_entropy with smoothing severance). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Replaced the manual per-element BCE accumulator with delegation to bce_with_logits_loss, which already implements the same numerically-stable formula max(x, 0) - x*t + log1p(exp(-|x|)) via composition through tensor_relu + tensor_abs + tensor_log1p + tensor_mean (all autograd-aware). For shape [batch, classes] both losses produce identical scalars (mean over all elements) and now both share gradient flow.

Added multilabel_soft_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_via_bce_with_logits regression test. Tests at x = ±1 to avoid the relu/abs corner at x=0 where FrankenTorch's sub-gradient convention differs slightly from the smooth sigmoid(x)-t form. ft-api lib tests: 1152.

title: Fix SafeTensors serialization to respect storage offsets
close_reason: Fix SafeTensors serialization for storage offsets/contiguity and add tests

title: Fail-close SafeTensors integer/bool dtype bridge in ft-serialize
description: The SafeTensors bridge maps I64/I32/BOOL into FrankenTorch dtypes on load, but load_safetensors_from_bytes returns DenseTensor and only reconstructs floating dtypes. Tighten the bridge so unsupported integer/bool dtypes fail closed with explicit diagnostics, add regression tests, and verify via rch.
close_reason: Completed

title: [profiling-software-performance] dedupe redundant ones-tensor allocations in tensor_atanh and focal_loss
description:
Two more compose-based ops allocate the same ones-tensor multiple times within a single call. Same pattern as cc2o (heaviside/pow_tensor) and d521 (sinc).

tensor_atanh (lib.rs:5419):
  let one  = full(shape, 1.0)?;
  let one2 = full(shape, 1.0)?;     // duplicate — used only by tensor_sub
  let half = full(shape, 0.5)?;
  ...
Both 'one' and 'one2' are passed to ops (tensor_add, tensor_sub) that only READ their operands; one allocation is enough.

focal_loss for ft-api (lib.rs:12382 / 12407 area):
  let ones1 = full(in_shape, 1.0)?; tensor_sub(ones1, p)
  let ones2 = full(in_shape, 1.0)?; tensor_sub(ones2, target)
  let ones3 = full(in_shape, 1.0)?; tensor_sub(ones3, p_t)
THREE allocations of the same ones-tensor, each consumed by one tensor_sub. -2 alloc + -2 tape nodes per focal_loss call.

For training loops that hammer atanh (in normalizing flows / hyperbolic embeddings) or focal_loss (in object detection heads), the wasted work compounds.

Acceptance:
- tensor_atanh uses one shared 'ones' across both tensor_add and tensor_sub (drop one2)
- focal_loss uses one shared 'ones' across all three tensor_sub call sites (drop ones2, ones3)
- existing tests still pass — no behavior change
- ft-api lib tests count unchanged
close_reason: Shipped: d667061. tensor_atanh shares 'one' across add/sub; focal_loss shares 'ones' across three tensor_sub call sites. -3 full() + -3 tape nodes total per call across both ops. 5 tests including gradient regressions pass.

title: tensor_linalg_vector_norm severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
tensor_linalg_vector_norm in ft-api (line 12741) extracts tensor_values, computes the chosen norm in plain f64, and rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(vec![norm], vec![1], false). This silently severs autograd through every vector_norm computation — used widely in normalization, loss functions, gradient clipping, etc.

Fix: compose through autograd-aware primitives. For each ord:
- ord = inf: tensor_abs + tensor_max (max is differentiable at the strict argmax)
- ord = -inf: tensor_abs + tensor_min
- ord = 0: non-differentiable (integer count) — keep severed but add fail-loud guard for requires_grad
- ord = p (positive finite): tensor_abs + tensor_pow(p) + tensor_sum + tensor_pow(1/p)

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_linalg_vector_norm (line 12741).

title: tensor_repeat_interleave severs autograd; sum-of-R-grad backward
description:
tensor_repeat_interleave at lib.rs:11097 takes a 1-D input and repeats each element R times consecutively. PyTorch's torch.repeat_interleave IS differentiable.

Backward: grad_input[i] = sum over j where j // R == i of grad_output[j]. Each input element gets the sum of the R consecutive output gradients that came from it.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_repeat_interleave in tensor_apply_function with sum-of-R backward: grad_input[i] = sum_{j: j / R == i} grad_output[j]. Each input element gets the sum of the R consecutive output gradients that came from it. Preserved the byte-size overflow check (output count alone can fit in usize even when out_len * 8 bytes overflows). Added repeat_interleave_propagates_gradient_via_sum_of_repeats regression test (x=[3,5], R=4 → grad=[4,4]). ft-api lib tests: 1165.

title: Implement scaled_dot_product_attention, baddbmm, addbmm
description: Add F.scaled_dot_product_attention (PyTorch 2.0+ attention primitive), baddbmm (batch add + batch matrix multiply), addbmm (add + batch matrix multiply). SDPA is the core building block for all modern transformer architectures. baddbmm is used internally by attention implementations.
close_reason: Implemented scaled_dot_product_attention (causal mask, dropout), baddbmm, addbmm. 10 tests passing.

title: Implement randint, randperm, multinomial
description: Add torch.randint (random integers in range), torch.randperm (random permutation), torch.multinomial (weighted sampling). Critical for: data loading (randperm for shuffling), language model sampling (multinomial for token selection), data augmentation (randint). All use the deterministic xoshiro256++ PRNG.
close_reason: Implemented randint (with range validation), randperm (Fisher-Yates), multinomial (with/without replacement, batched). 9 tests passing.

title: porting: add tensor_clip / inverse / subtract / multiply / divide aliases
description:
PyTorch keeps numpy-style aliases for several common ops:
  - torch.clip == torch.clamp
  - torch.inverse == torch.linalg.inv (deprecated but still in active use)
  - torch.subtract == torch.sub
  - torch.multiply == torch.mul
  - torch.divide == torch.div
Add one-line wrapper functions in ft-api so users can port code that uses either name without manual translation.
close_reason: Added 5 numpy-style aliases in ft-api: tensor_clip (== tensor_clamp), tensor_subtract (== tensor_sub), tensor_multiply (== tensor_mul), tensor_divide (== tensor_div), tensor_inverse (== tensor_linalg_inv). All one-line wrappers. 2 unit tests verify equivalence to underlying ops. ft-api now at 1294 lib tests.

title: porting: add functional kl_div_loss with reduction + log_target options
description: torch.nn.functional.kl_div(input, target, reduction='mean', log_target=False) is a more flexible variant than the KLDivLoss nn::Module wrapper (which is mean-only, no log_target). Add kl_div_loss to ft-api supporting reduction in {none, sum, mean, batchmean} and log_target. Compose through autograd-aware tensor_log + tensor_sub + tensor_mul + appropriate reduction. Useful for distillation, VAE losses, and policy distillation in RL.
close_reason: Added kl_div_loss in ft-api: KL(target || input) with reduction in {none, sum, mean, batchmean} and log_target option. Two formulas: log_target=false → target * (log(target+eps) - input); log_target=true → exp(target) * (target - input). Composes through autograd-aware tensor_log + tensor_sub + tensor_mul + reduction. 6 unit tests cover identity (KL of P||P ≈ 0), positive divergence on different distributions, log_target formula, batchmean divisor (sum/leading_dim), gradient backward (-target_i for log_target=false), and unknown-reduction rejection. ft-api now at 1266 lib tests.

title: Implement tensor_cdist and tensor_pdist
description: Add cdist (batch pairwise distances) and pdist (within-tensor pairwise distances). cdist: torch.cdist(x1, x2, p=2.0). pdist: torch.nn.functional.pdist(input, p=2). Used in metric learning, clustering, few-shot learning.

title: porting: add tensor_nanvar + tensor_nanstd for torch parity
description:
Companion to tensor_nansum/nanmean (8q4o). Add NaN-aware variance and standard deviation:
  - nanvar(x, correction): variance over non-NaN elements; correction=0 → biased (divide by count), correction=1 → Bessel (divide by count-1, default in torch).
  - nanstd(x, correction) = sqrt(nanvar(x, correction)).
Compose through tensor_isnan + tensor_where (zero out NaN in both x and the diff term) + nanmean + sub + mul + sum + div.
close_reason: Added tensor_nanvar(input, correction) and tensor_nanstd(input, correction) in ft-api as the variance/std companion to nansum/nanmean (8q4o). Composes through tensor_isnan + tensor_where (zeros NaN in both x and the diff term) + nanmean + sub + mul + sum + div. correction=0 → biased divisor; correction=1 → Bessel-corrected (torch default); correction > count_non_nan → 0 divisor → NaN result. 6 unit tests cover biased/unbiased correctness against [1, NaN, 3, NaN, 5] (mean=3, sum_sq=8 → biased var=8/3, unbiased=4, std=2), all-NaN edge case, single-non-NaN-with-Bessel edge case, and analytic gradient regression (∂var/∂a = 0.5(a-c) for the 2-non-NaN case). ft-api now at 1315 lib tests.

title: Add E2E training test for EmbeddingBag → Linear classifier
description:
Add an end-to-end training test that exercises the recently fixed EmbeddingBag (frankentorch-y8le) in a realistic classifier scenario. Verifies gradients flow through EmbeddingBag → Linear → cross_entropy → Adam.step for many iterations.

Setup:
- Vocab of 6 token embeddings, dim=4
- 4 training examples: bags [0, 1] and [2, 3] map to class 0; bags [4] and [5] map to class 1
- EmbeddingBag(mode=Mean) → Linear(4 → 2)
- Cross-entropy loss; Adam lr=0.1 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1 (10x reduction)

Catches regressions in EmbeddingBag autograd flow that unit-level gradient tests might miss.

title: [parity-gap] ConvTranspose1d exposes rank-2 bias unlike PyTorch
description:
Review source: six-hour audit after a9893b2/79730d8/fd35f3b expanded ConvTranspose1d fan-in and conformance coverage.

Defect: ConvTranspose1d::new allocates bias with shape [1, out_channels], so ConvTranspose1d::bias() exposes a rank-2 parameter. PyTorch nn.ConvTranspose1d exposes bias as shape [out_channels]. This is user-observable through parameter traversal/state dict shape and diverges from ConvTranspose2d/3d in this crate, which already allocate [out_channels]. The stale forward workaround comment still says tensor_expand cannot add dims, but a9893b2 added higher-rank expand support, so the module can keep PyTorch's rank-1 bias and reshape only inside forward.

Concrete repro steps:
1. In crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs, construct ConvTranspose1d::new(&mut session, 2, 3, 3, 1, 0, 0, true).
2. Call session.tensor_shape(deconv.bias().unwrap()).
3. Actual FrankenTorch shape is [1, 3].
4. Upstream PyTorch: torch.nn.ConvTranspose1d(2, 3, 3).bias.shape is torch.Size([3]).

Expected fix: allocate ConvTranspose1d bias as [out_channels], keep forward reshaping to [1, out_channels, 1] before expansion, and add a parity/conformance test that locks the public bias shape.
close_reason: Fixed ConvTranspose1d public bias parameter shape to match PyTorch [out_channels], kept forward reshape for broadcast, and added ft-nn plus ft-conformance parity tests. Validated with targeted rch cargo tests, fmt, package check, clippy, and UBS review.

title: RaptorQ sidecar generation accepts unbounded repair symbol counts
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-serialize/src/lib.rs and crates/ft-conformance/src/bin/run_raptorq_durability_pipeline.rs. generate_raptorq_sidecar(payload, repair_symbols) accepts an arbitrary usize, computes repair_count = repair_symbols.max(...).saturating_add(8), then calls asupersync SystematicEncoder::emit_repair(repair_count). asupersync 0.3.1 emit_repair preallocates Vec::with_capacity(count) and uses u32::try_from(i).expect("repair count exceeds u32") / checked_add(...).expect(...) inside the loop. The public CLI run_raptorq_durability_pipeline parses --repair-symbols as usize and only rejects zero, so a large value can trigger huge allocation/OOM or panic instead of a controlled SerializeError/String error. Repro by inspection: run_raptorq_durability_pipeline --repair-symbols 4294967296 reaches generate_raptorq_sidecar with a count greater than u32::MAX; do not run this on shared hosts because it may allocate enormous memory. Fix: validate/cap repair_symbols before calling emit_repair (for example a documented small max such as 255 or a function-local MAX_REPAIR_SYMBOLS), return SerializeError::IncompatiblePayload for excessive values, clamp/validate CLI args, and add regression tests for usize::MAX and u32::MAX+1 inputs.
close_reason: Added MAX_RAPTORQ_REPAIR_SYMBOLS = 1024 (user-input cap) and an internal RAPTORQ_REPAIR_COUNT_HARD_CAP = 65536 to bound the computed repair_count after k_prime padding + headroom. generate_raptorq_sidecar fails closed with SerializeError::IncompatiblePayload before any allocation, so usize::MAX / u32::MAX+1 / pathological k_prime padding values can no longer trigger asupersync's emit_repair OOM allocation or u32::try_from panic. CLI run_raptorq_durability_pipeline mirrors the input cap for a user-facing diagnostic. Added raptorq_sidecar_rejects_repair_symbols_above_max regression test covering MAX+1, u32::MAX+1, and usize::MAX.

title: [code-review] preserve non-F64 dtype in autograd shape ops
close_reason: Preserved typed storage for autograd reshape/squeeze/unsqueeze/narrow and added F16/BF16/Complex64 regression tests.

title: Audit unchecked shape product multiplications in ft-api
description: Found many shape.iter().product() in ft-api runtime paths without overflow checks. In release builds this can wrap, leading to silent shape corruption or massive allocations. Audit and replace with checked_shape_numel/checked_mul where appropriate, add tests for overflow error paths.
close_reason: Replaced unchecked shape products with overflow-checked math in ft-api

title: Add ParameterList and ParameterDict containers to ft-nn
description: Add ParameterList and ParameterDict module containers. Essential for dynamic architectures and meta-programming patterns. ParameterList holds parameters by index, ParameterDict by key.
close_reason: Implemented ParameterList and ParameterDict with full Module trait, insert/get/iter/append, named_parameter_slots. 8 new tests passing.

title: [code-review][reliability] four more functions emit opaque dim-out-of-range diagnostics — migrate to KernelError::InvalidDimension
description:
After oagi shipped tensor_glu's migration to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim }, four more functions in ft-api still emit static-reason 'X: dim out of range' diagnostics that don't carry the offending dim or the input rank:

  functional_normalize    lib.rs:6618
  index_add               lib.rs:9062
  index_copy              lib.rs:9217
  index_fill              lib.rs:9344

Sister ops (tensor_diag, tensor_diag_embed, tensor_dot, tensor_glu after oagi) use KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim } for the identical failure mode. Display includes both values, so callers can debug without printing their own shape.

Concrete repro: a user calling functional_normalize(x, p=2, dim=5) on a rank-3 tensor sees only 'normalize: dim out of range', forcing them to print x.shape from upstream code. The InvalidDimension variant emits 'invalid dimension 5 for tensor of rank 3' standalone.

Acceptance:
- all four functions migrated to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim } at the dim >= ndim guard
- existing tests still pass (the error-type change preserves the Result-is-Err contract)
- ft-api lib tests count unchanged
close_reason: Shipped: 2d68bd0. functional_normalize / index_add / index_copy / index_fill all migrated to KernelError::InvalidDimension { dim, ndim }. Diagnostics now carry both values; matches the policy used by tensor_glu (oagi) / tensor_diag / tensor_dot. 36 affected tests pass.

title: AlphaDropout in ft-nn severs autograd; SELU networks broken
description:
AlphaDropout at ft-nn/src/lib.rs:2146 implements its forward by extracting input values, applying the alpha-dropout transform in plain f64, and rebuilding a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.nn.AlphaDropout IS differentiable. Severs autograd through every AlphaDropout layer.

AlphaDropout is paired with SELU activations to maintain mean/variance through dropout — used in self-normalizing networks (Klambauer et al. 2017). Models using SELU + AlphaDropout couldn't actually train.

Math: y = a * (mask * x + (1 - mask) * sat) + a * p * sat
where mask is the random keep-or-drop mask, sat is the saturation value, a is a precomputed scale.

Composable through autograd primitives: mask leaf (non-grad) + tensor_mul (autograd) for the input branch, constant offsets via full + tensor_add for the rest.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed AlphaDropout forward through autograd-aware primitives: random mask leaf (non-grad) + tensor_mul against input + tensor_sub for inverse mask + scaled drop branch + affine offset. The transform y = a * (mask * x + (1-mask) * sat) + a*p*sat is preserved exactly but the autograd tape now flows through input. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: Fail closed on invalid multinomial weights
close_reason: Completed

title: [parity-gap] tensor_pow_tensor(0, 0) returns NaN, torch returns 1
close_reason: Shipped: 7eb74c0. Routed (input==0 AND exponent==0) through tensor_where to a constant 1, matching torch.pow / numpy.power / IEEE 754 convention. New regression pow_tensor_zero_to_zero_is_one mixes 0^0 with vanilla cases. Existing gradient regression unaffected.

title: Add E2E training test for MultiheadAttention self-attention
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression test that wires MultiheadAttention (self-attention) into a small training task. Validates the q/k/v/out projection chain + softmax + matmul through autograd.

Setup:
- 1x4x8 input sequence (batch=1, seq=4, embed=8)
- MultiheadAttention(embed_dim=8, num_heads=2)
- MSE against a fixed 1x4x8 target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: tensor_scatter_reduce amax/amin autograd via argmax-routing masks + scatter_add
description:
After uzca added mean autograd, only prod/amax/amin still fail loud. amax and amin can be implemented via argmax-routing:

1. Compute argmax_per_position in plain f64 (non-diff)
2. mask_input[i] = 1 if input[i] is the max (won), else 0
3. mask_src[j] = 1 if src[j] is the max at position index[j], else 0
4. result = scatter_add(mul(mask_input, input), dim, index, mul(mask_src, src))

For each output position, exactly one of input[i] or one src[j]→i 'wins'; the other terms are zeroed by the masks. Backward correctly routes gradient to the winning element via mul backward. Same pattern works for amin (find argmin instead).

prod still needs special handling (divide-back is unstable at zero); defer.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_scatter_reduce (line 16249).

title: Fail closed on non-finite values in index-like ft-api ops
close_reason: Completed

title: pad_sequence severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
pad_sequence in ft-nn (line ~13596) extracts tensor_values from each input sequence, builds a flat output buffer with placeholder padding values, then rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(output, out_shape, false). This silently severs autograd through pad_sequence — gradients can't flow back to any input sequence. Breaks training for any RNN/LSTM/GRU pipeline that uses torch.nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence on variable-length data.

Fix: each sequence can be padded to max_len via tensor_pad (autograd-aware), then stacked along the new batch dimension. The padding value is configurable; if non-zero, compose: padded = tensor_pad(seq, [...], 0.0) + (1 - mask) * padding_value where mask is the non-grad tensor marking the original positions. For padding_value=0.0 (the common case), tensor_pad alone suffices.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs pad_sequence (line 13596 region).

title: tensor_cartesian_prod severs autograd; per-tensor scatter backward
description:
tensor_cartesian_prod at lib.rs:4065 takes 1-D tensors and emits all combinations as a 2-D output. Each output cell is a copy of one input element via a deterministic odometer mapping. PyTorch's torch.cartesian_prod IS differentiable.

Backward: for each input tensor k with N_k entries,
   grad_input_k[i] = sum over rows where indices[k] == i of grad_output[row, k]

Each input element appears in (total_rows / N_k) rows, so each contribution is sum of that many grad entries. Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this scatter-per-input backward.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cartesian_prod in tensor_apply_function with per-tensor scatter backward. Pre-computed (row, k) → input_index_in_tensor_k mapping is captured by both forward (gather) and backward (scatter) closures so the routing is deterministic and free of per-call recomputation. Backward returns Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input tensor. Added cartesian_prod_propagates_gradient_to_each_input regression test (a=[1,2], b=[3,4] → grad_a=[2,2], grad_b=[2,2] under sum loss). ft-api lib tests: 1167.

title: Harden scheduler state_dict numeric field loading
close_reason: Completed

title: functional_dropout severs autograd; mask is supposed to flow gradient through input
description:
functional_dropout at lib.rs:5199 generates random mask values, multiplies in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd through dropout. Every transformer / convnet training that uses dropout would have gradients of input dropped to zero by the dropout layer's masking. PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.dropout is differentiable: the mask is sampled per-forward (non-differentiable random) but the multiplication input * mask * scale carries gradient through input.

Fix: replace the manual values+leaf assembly with composition through autograd-aware tensor_mul:
   mask_vals = 0 where dropped, scale=1/(1-p) where surviving (random per-element)
   mask = tensor_variable(mask_vals, shape, false)   # non-grad mask is correct
   output = tensor_mul(input, mask)                  # autograd-aware

Same observable forward behavior, but the autograd tape is preserved for the input.

Critical severity — this affects every dropout-using training run. Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a / x9cr). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Replaced functional_dropout's manual mask-and-scale body with composition through tensor_mul against a non-grad random mask. The mask values are 0 (dropped) or scale = 1/(1-p) (kept), generated per-element via the RNG; multiplication preserves the autograd tape so input gradients flow as expected. Same observable forward behavior, but training paths through dropout layers now actually train.

Critical-severity fix: every dropout-using transformer / convnet training was previously getting zero gradient downstream of any dropout layer, making training a no-op for the affected sub-network.

Added functional_dropout_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (n=100, p=0.5 → grad entries are 0 or 1/(1-p) = 2 element-wise; sanity check that at least 10 survive). ft-api lib tests: 1155.

title: Add bernoulli and poisson random sampling ops
description: Implement tensor_randint, tensor_randperm, tensor_bernoulli, tensor_multinomial in ft-api. These are commonly used PyTorch tensor creation operations needed for drop-in parity.
close_reason: Implemented bernoulli, bernoulli_p, and poisson ops in ft-api with 8 tests passing. randint, randperm, multinomial already existed.

title: Add distributed training support (DDP/FSDP equivalents)
description:
FrankenTorch currently has no distributed-process orchestration, gradient synchronization, sharded parameter management, collective communication surface, or optimizer/state partitioning comparable to PyTorch DDP/FSDP.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Define distributed process-group / collective abstractions
2. Add replicated gradient synchronization for DDP-style training
3. Add parameter/state sharding for FSDP-style execution
4. Preserve deterministic replay and audit evidence across multi-rank execution
5. Add conformance scenarios for cross-rank gradient equivalence and failure handling

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can explicitly mark distributed training as deferred rather than implying it is already covered by the current CPU eager-mode slice.

title: Fix HingeEmbeddingLoss autograd via tensor_where + relu [child of cq0b]
description:
HingeEmbeddingLoss::forward_hinge at lib.rs:11003 computes elementwise:
   loss_i = x_i        if y_i > 0
          = max(0, margin - x_i)  otherwise
Composable: y_pos_mask = (y > 0), then mask * x + (1 - mask) * relu(margin - x). Or tensor_where over the same condition.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed HingeEmbeddingLoss through tensor_relu + mask-based blend (mask is non-grad target-derived; pos_branch = mask*input; neg_branch = (1-mask)*relu(margin-input); loss = mean(pos+neg)). All ops autograd-aware. ft-nn lib tests: 517.

title: Add metamorphic property test: pack→pad_packed round-trip preserves values
description:
Add a property test that wires pack_padded_sequence → pad_packed_sequence end-to-end on randomized variable-length sequences and asserts the round-trip recovers each sequence's values bit-exactly within its real length. Validates that the recently-fixed pack/pad_packed autograd composition (frankentorch-tp3r) preserves data through the gather/scatter pattern.

Setup:
- Generate 3 sequences of random lengths in [1, 5] from a fixed pad-then-stack input
- pack_padded_sequence then pad_packed_sequence
- Assert: each sequence's first  values match the input bit-exactly

title: tensor_nonzero on 0-d scalar always returns shape [0, 0]
description:
FrankenTorchSession::tensor_nonzero() at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:10260 silently returns shape [0, 0] for any 0-d (scalar) input — including non-zero scalars where PyTorch returns shape [1, 0].

Root cause at line 10309:
    let num_nonzero = indices.len().checked_div(ndim).unwrap_or(0);
When ndim == 0 (scalar), the inner stride loop runs 0 times so 'indices' stays empty for any value, and 0.checked_div(0).unwrap_or(0) = 0 — losing the 'is non-zero' signal entirely.

PyTorch parity:
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(5.0)) -> shape [1, 0]
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(0.0)) -> shape [0, 0]
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(float('nan'))) -> shape [1, 0]  (NaN treated as nonzero)

Fix: track non-zero count explicitly instead of inferring it from indices.len() / ndim, and also clean up the related tensor_nonzero_as_tuple which currently returns an empty Vec for any scalar (matches PyTorch '() tuple' but worth documenting).
close_reason: tensor_nonzero now tracks num_nonzero explicitly so 0-d scalars produce the correct PyTorch-equivalent shape: non-zero scalar -> [1, 0], zero scalar -> [0, 0], NaN scalar -> [1, 0] (NaN treated as nonzero). Previously indices.len().checked_div(0).unwrap_or(0) collapsed all scalars to [0, 0]. 3 new scalar tests added; all 1082 ft-api tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

title: Add tensor_select, tensor_take_along_dim, tensor_scatter_reduce
close_reason: Implemented tensor_select, tensor_take_along_dim, tensor_scatter_reduce with 8 tests

title: Harden ft-serialize decode paths against panic-prone byte conversions
close_reason: Completed

title: porting: add tensor_pow_tensor for torch.pow(x, exponent_tensor) parity
description:
Element-wise power with a TENSOR exponent (rather than scalar — that's tensor_pow). Compose via exp(exponent * log(input)). Autograd-aware on both inputs:
  d/dinput = result * exponent / input
  d/dexponent = result * log(input)
Well-defined for input > 0; for input ≤ 0 the formula produces NaN/inf (matches torch's behavior for non-integer exponents on negative bases — torch's signed-integer-exponent path is a separate slow case).
close_reason: Added tensor_pow_tensor in ft-api: element-wise input^exponent with both operands as same-shape tensors. Composes via exp(exponent * log(input)). Autograd-aware on both: ∂y/∂x = y*e/x and ∂y/∂e = y*log(x), both from chain rule on the exp/mul/log composition. Negative bases produce NaN (matches torch.pow on non-integer exponents over negative bases — the signed-integer exponent slow path is intentionally not replicated). 4 unit tests cover known values, gradient regression at (x=4, e=3) verifying ∂/∂x=48 and ∂/∂e=64*ln(4), shape-mismatch rejection, and negative-base NaN behavior. ft-api now at 1319 lib tests.

title: weight_norm_reconstruct severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild
description:
weight_norm_reconstruct in ft-nn (line 13718) extracts tensor_values from g and v, computes w = g * v / ||v|| in plain f64, and rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(w_values, v_shape, false). This silently severs autograd: gradients can't flow back to either g (magnitude) or v (direction), breaking training for any model using nn.utils.weight_norm (WaveNet, certain GANs, normalizing-flow variants).

Fix: compose via autograd primitives. Math:
  w[i, ..., dim_idx, ..., j] = g[dim_idx] * v[i, ..., dim_idx, ..., j] / ||v||[dim_idx]
where ||v||[dim_idx] = sqrt(sum over non-dim axes of v^2).

Implementation: permute v so dim is first, reshape to [dim_size, others], compute v*v, sum_dim along axis 1, sqrt, clamp_min(eps); broadcast g and norm to v's permuted shape; divide; multiply; reshape and permute back to v_shape.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs weight_norm_reconstruct (line 13718)

title: Fail closed on non-finite one_hot indices
close_reason: Completed

title: [parity-gap] tensor_expand rejects PyTorch higher-rank expand
close_reason: Validated and fixed in review mode: F32 nan reductions pass targeted ft-api nan tests; tensor_expand now supports PyTorch higher-rank expand with forward/backward ft-api tests and subprocess conformance coverage.

title: porting: add tensor_nansum and tensor_nanmean for torch parity
description: Add tensor_nansum and tensor_nanmean ops mirroring torch.nansum / torch.nanmean. Both treat NaN as missing data (zero contribution). Compose through autograd-aware tensor_isnan + tensor_where so gradients flow only to non-NaN positions. nanmean uses the count of non-NaN elements as the divisor. Useful for ML workflows with masked / missing data.
close_reason: Added tensor_nansum and tensor_nanmean ops mirroring torch.nansum / torch.nanmean. nansum: composes through tensor_isnan + tensor_where(zeros) + tensor_sum so NaN positions contribute 0 to the sum and 0 to the gradient (where routes upstream grad to the all-zero constant which has no requires_grad). nanmean: same as nansum then divides by (numel - nan_count). ±inf passes through unchanged (matches PyTorch — only NaN is masked). All-NaN edge case returns NaN via 0/0 divisor. 6 unit tests added: nansum_treats_nan_as_zero, nansum_passes_through_inf, nansum_propagates_gradient_to_non_nan_positions, nanmean_divides_by_non_nan_count, nanmean_all_nan_returns_nan, nanmean_propagates_gradient_scaled_by_one_over_count. ft-api now at 1223 lib tests.

title: Fix unchecked negative index in tensor_scatter_reduce
close_reason: Added validate_index_tensor_values + negative index wrapping to tensor_scatter_reduce. 2 regression tests.

title: Make with_no_grad and with_enable_grad actually panic-safe
description: and  are documented as panic-safe RAII equivalents, but they currently leave grad-enabled stack state dirty if the closure unwinds before the matching exit call. Restore state on panic and add regression coverage in ft-api.
close_reason: Already fixed: with_no_grad/with_enable_grad use catch_unwind with proper cleanup. Two panic-safety regression tests already exist and pass (with_no_grad_restores_state_after_panic, with_enable_grad_restores_outer_no_grad_after_panic).

title: Add metamorphic invariant tests for softmax/log_softmax/transpose/inverse equivalences
description:
Existing fuzz harness in ft-conformance covers 4 ops (abs, add, sum, matmul) with shape/finiteness assertions. There are no metamorphic equivalence tests — checking that two algebraically-equivalent paths produce the same output within ULP tolerance.

Catch-cases this would lock down:
1. exp(log_softmax(x)) ≈ softmax(x)   — composition equivalence
2. log(softmax(x)) ≈ log_softmax(x)   — direct vs explicit
3. softmax(x).sum() ≈ 1.0             — probability invariant
4. softmax(x + c) ≈ softmax(x)        — translation invariance
5. transpose(transpose(x)) ≡ x        — involution
6. matmul(I, A) ≈ A                   — identity invariance
7. matmul(A, A.inv()) ≈ I             — inverse invariance (well-conditioned A)
8. (a + b).sum() ≈ a.sum() + b.sum()  — sum linearity

Each can be a proptest with random valid inputs. The log_softmax precision bug fixed under frankentorch-ebrb would have been caught by (1) and (2) — that's the kind of bug these target.

Filed during /testing-metamorphic round of the multi-pass cycle.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Added 4 metamorphic property tests inside the existing #[cfg(feature='fuzz')] proptest! block in ft-conformance:
  - fuzz_metamorphic_log_softmax_equals_log_of_softmax (the case that would have caught frankentorch-ebrb at random inputs)
  - fuzz_metamorphic_softmax_sums_to_one (probability-distribution invariant)
  - fuzz_metamorphic_softmax_translation_invariance (softmax(x + c) == softmax(x))
  - fuzz_metamorphic_double_transpose_is_identity (bit-exact involution)
ULP-tolerant comparators with absolute-floor fallbacks. ft-conformance lib tests with --features fuzz: 135 passing.

title: Add TorchScript/JIT and torch.compile-style graph execution
description:
FrankenTorch currently executes an eager CPU-first tensor/autograd stack. It does not expose graph capture, TorchScript serialization/execution, ahead-of-time lowering, or `torch.compile`-style graph optimization/runtime selection.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Define graph capture / IR boundaries for eager programs
2. Add TorchScript-like serialization or a compatible alternative representation
3. Add compile-time graph lowering / optimization pipeline for `torch.compile`-style workflows
4. Preserve observable tensor semantics and DAC evidence under compiled execution
5. Add conformance scenarios comparing eager vs compiled outputs and gradients

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can explicitly mark JIT / `torch.compile` work as deferred instead of silently omitting a major PyTorch subsystem.

Three more fuzz_corpus_prop tests in ft-conformance (proptest! block):

1. fuzz_corpus_prop_unary_relu_is_non_negative — for any input, dispatch_tensor_unary_contiguous_f64(Relu, ...) output is ≥ 0 element-wise.

title: Fix scatter_reduce shape validation and f32 output
description: scatter_reduce currently assumes index/src shapes align with input and uses f64-only storage, which can panic on rank/shape mismatch and silently upcast f32 outputs. Add rank/shape/dtype checks and ensure f32 outputs stay f32; add tests for shape mismatches and dtype preservation.

title: tensor_bincount severs autograd through weights tensor
description:
ft-api tensor_bincount (line 3651) extracts both input and weight tensor_values, builds the bin sums in plain f64, and rebuilds via tensor_variable(_, _, false). The input (bin indices) is integer-valued and intrinsically non-differentiable, but PyTorch's bincount IS differentiable through the weights — silently severing here breaks training for any use of weighted bincount in custom losses (focal loss in detection, class re-weighting, etc.).

Fix: compose via tensor_scatter_add. Build a zeros tensor of shape [n_bins], use input (cast to f64 index tensor) as scatter index, and weights as src. The unweighted case still returns counts (use ones tensor as src).

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_bincount (line 3651).

title: Add E2E training test for Sequential composition
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression that wires Linear → ReLU → Linear into a Sequential and trains end-to-end. Validates Sequential's parameters() collection across composed children, plus the chained forward+backward.

Setup:
- Sequential([Linear(4 → 8), ReLU, Linear(8 → 1)])
- 4-sample regression batch
- MSE + Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: Add PolynomialLR and ConstantLR schedulers
